config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk menindas orang yang lemah atau minoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, bukan dengan mengorbankan martabat dan kebebasan pribadi seenaknya. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru terbentuk ketika hak individu dilindungi dengan serius.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States that logic has too often been used to justify trampling minorities and unpopular voices. Public health, safety, and basic order matter, but they have to be balanced against constitutional rights and personal dignity, not treated as a blank check. If "society" is allowed to override individuals too easily, then the people with the least power usually pay the price. A healthy society is one that protects the common good while keeping strong limits on what the majority can demand from the individual.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembungkaman, diskriminasi, atau tekanan terhadap orang yang tidak punya kuasa, padahal justru di situlah negara dan masyarakat harus paling hati-hati. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tidak boleh dikorbankan seenaknya. Kalau hak individu lemah, yang sering menang bukan masyarakat, melainkan mayoritas yang paling keras suaranya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, that idea has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially against minorities and dissenters. I do think there are limited cases where public safety or emergency health measures can restrict individual freedom, but those limits have to be narrow, accountable, and temporary. If society can override rights whenever it claims a collective need, then individual liberty becomes fragile and conditional.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diutamakan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari konteks Indonesia, dalih “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembungkaman, diskriminasi, atau tekanan terhadap kelompok yang tidak punya kuasa, jadi saya tidak percaya itu aman jika dijadikan prioritas utama. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang jelas, dengan pengecualian yang benar-benar sempit, diawasi, dan sementara. Kalau tidak, yang disebut kepentingan masyarakat sering kali hanya suara mayoritas yang paling keras, bukan keadilan yang sesungguhnya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general principle. From an American point of view, that kind of argument has too often been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially when the people affected are minorities or unpopular dissenters. Society absolutely has legitimate interests in safety, public health, and order, but those interests need strict limits and accountability rather than open-ended power over individual rights. In practice, when "the common good" is treated as automatically more important, the people with the least power are usually the ones who lose the most.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
